steps.title: Spot the series structure
steps.content:
The verb “registering” is followed by a blank, then the word “and” and the final item “16°C at 5:00 p.m.” This shows we need to list two items before the “and,” with commas separating each item in the series.

Thus the blank must contain two temperature‑time expressions separated by a comma, and it must end with a comma so the series can continue.

steps.title: Apply the series‑comma rule
steps.content:
Choice D writes “15.4°C at 1:00 a.m., 18.6°C at 11:30 a.m.,” – it places a comma between the two items and adds a trailing comma, exactly what the series requires.

The other three choices either omit the internal comma (A and B) or misplace commas that split the temperature from its time (C), breaking the parallel structure.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
